assignments.fact: Community-acquired pneumonia, right lower lobe, probably bacterial
assignments.rationale: The assessment states "Community-acquired pneumonia, right lower lobe, probably bacterial." Among the candidates, "Unspecified bacterial pneumonia" best matches the documented bacterial etiology.

assignments.description: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with (acute) exacerbation
assignments.rationale: The note explicitly documents "Acute exacerbation of COPD," which directly maps to "Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with (acute) exacerbation."
The assessment states "Acute hypoxic respiratory failure" and the vitals show hypoxia (SpO2 89% on 2 L), supporting "Acute respiratory failure with hypoxia."

assignments.description: [SYNTHETIC] Radiologic examination chest two views frontal and lateral
assignments.rationale: The imaging section documents "Chest X-ray two views," which matches "Radiologic examination chest two views frontal and lateral."

assignments.fact: Admit to medical floor with telemetry.
assignments.description: [SYNTHETIC] Hospital inpatient or observation care - initial - high complexity
assignments.rationale: The plan says "Admit to medical floor with telemetry." This is an initial hospital inpatient admission, and the documented acute pneumonia/COPD/respiratory failure makes high-complexity inpatient care the best match.

assignments.fact: Nebulizer treatment with albuterol/ipratropium every 4 hours.
assignments.description: [SYNTHETIC] Nebulizer treatment for the administration of an inhalation solution
assignments.rationale: The plan explicitly orders "Nebulizer treatment with albuterol/ipratropium every 4 hours," which matches nebulizer treatment for inhalation solution administration.
The chief complaint includes "dyspnea" along with cough and fever; among the available symptom codes, "Shortness of breath" is the best match for the documented breathing complaint.
"Productive cough" is best captured by "Abnormal sputum" since the note documents productive sputum/cough rather than a specific cough chronicity.
The HPI documents "subjective fevers to 38.7 C," which maps directly to "Fever, unspecified."
The HPI states "progressive dyspnea," which matches "Shortness of breath."
The past medical history explicitly lists "Essential hypertension," which maps directly to essential (primary) hypertension.
The past medical history documents "Right hip osteoarthritis." In the absence of a different subtype, the best match is unilateral primary osteoarthritis, right hip.

verdicts.note: Evidence clearly documents 'Chest X-ray two views' in imaging section, which directly supports the CPT code for radiologic examination chest two views frontal and lateral.

Plan documents initial inpatient admission to medical floor; patient presents with acute pneumonia, COPD exacerbation, and respiratory failure—clinical complexity supports high-complexity inpatient care code.
Plan explicitly orders 'Nebulizer treatment with albuterol/ipratropium every 4 hours,' which directly supports the nebulizer treatment code for inhalation solution administration.
